attacking_style: Not an attacker. Pure domestique role focused on tempo-setting and positioning work, typically pulling until climb base or critical points before dropping back.
team_tactics: Executes textbook leadout abandonment strategy: pulls hard on flat/rolling terrain to position team, sacrifices himself at decisive moments (climb base), occasionally bridges gaps when team needs numerical presence in breaks.
terrain_preference: Rolling terrain and cobbled classics where his power can control pace and create numerical advantages. Exposed on climbs where he drops after completing pacing duties.
vulnerability: Cannot finish off the work on technical finishes (Omloop Het Nieuwsblad - had numbers but wrong finishers). Drops immediately once climbing begins. Risky bike handling noted on twisty descents.
key_patterns: Textbook sacrifice domestique: pulls until climb base then drops back (2+ observations)
Deceuninck-QuickStep uses him for numerical advantage in breaks, not as finisher (2+ observations)
Executes leadout/tempo work on flat and rolling terrain before critical selection points (3+ observations)
Team positioning specialist: bridges gaps and controls pace before handing off to climbers (2+ observations)
form_trajectory: positive: evolved from pure flatland domestique to more versatile patron bridging gaps on rolling terrain by 2022
